description: Complete first-year university macroeconomics covering national income accounting, unemployment and inflation, aggregate demand and supply, fiscal and monetary policy, IS-LM model, Phillips curve, international trade and finance, economic growth, and development economics. Designed to provide a rigorous foundation for intermediate macroeconomics and economics majors.

topics.name: Unit 1: Foundations of Macroeconomics
topics.description: Scarcity, opportunity cost, production possibilities curve, comparative advantage, and circular flow model

topics.name: Unit 2: Measuring Economic Performance
topics.description: GDP (nominal and real), GDP components, GDP deflator, unemployment types and calculation, natural rate of unemployment, CPI, inflation costs, and Okun's Law

topics.name: Unit 3: Aggregate Demand and Supply
topics.description: AD curve, SRAS curve, LRAS curve, shifts in each, macroeconomic equilibrium, recessionary and inflationary gaps, self-correction, and sticky wages/prices

topics.name: Unit 4: Introduction to Macro Policy
topics.description: Macroeconomic policy goals, fiscal policy basics, monetary policy basics, and policy trade-offs

topics.name: Unit 5: Fiscal Policy In-Depth
topics.description: Government spending and taxation, spending and tax multipliers, balanced budget multiplier, expansionary and contractionary policy, crowding out, automatic stabilizers, government debt and deficits, and Ricardian equivalence

topics.name: Unit 6: Money, Banking, and Financial Systems
topics.description: Functions and characteristics of money, financial system structure, fractional reserve banking, money multiplier, Federal Reserve structure, and central bank operations

topics.name: Unit 7: Money Supply Measures and Money Demand
topics.description: M1 and M2 money supply, liquidity preference theory of money demand, factors affecting money demand, money market equilibrium, quantity theory of money (MV = PY), and Fisher equation (real vs. nominal interest rates)

topics.name: Unit 8: Monetary Policy In-Depth
topics.description: Monetary policy tools (open market operations, discount rate, reserve requirements), federal funds rate, expansionary and contractionary policy, transmission mechanism, quantitative easing, and monetary vs. fiscal policy comparison

topics.name: Unit 9: The IS-LM Model
topics.description: IS-LM assumptions, IS curve (goods market equilibrium), LM curve (money market equilibrium), simultaneous equilibrium, fiscal policy in IS-LM, monetary policy in IS-LM, policy mix, and slope extremes (liquidity trap and classical case)

topics.name: Unit 10: Phillips Curve and Stabilization Policy
topics.description: Short-run Phillips curve, long-run Phillips curve (vertical), role of expectations (adaptive vs. rational), shifts in the Phillips curve, disinflation and sacrifice ratio, stabilization policy, and policy lags

topics.name: Unit 11: International Trade
topics.description: Benefits of international trade, tariffs, quotas, arguments for trade protection, and balance of payments (current and financial accounts)

topics.name: Unit 12: Exchange Rates and Foreign Exchange
topics.description: Nominal and real exchange rates, supply and demand in forex, appreciation and depreciation, fixed vs. floating exchange rates, and purchasing power parity

topics.name: Unit 13: Economic Growth and Productivity
topics.description: Defining economic growth, productivity and growth, Solow growth model basics (capital accumulation and steady state), factors affecting growth (savings, population, technology), and convergence hypothesis

topics.name: Unit 14: Development and Global Economics
topics.description: Developed vs. developing economies, obstacles to growth (corruption, infrastructure, institutions), foreign aid and investment, globalization debate, and global financial crises
